channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Greenland, Panama fiercely reject Trump’s ambitions in address to Congress
item.description: Greenland and Panama made clear to President Donald Trump that his ambitions in their countries are unwelcome after he against addressed them in a speech to Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance suggests other industries won't get tariff carve-outs
item.description: Vice President Vance on Wednesday said that industries other than U.S. automakers have asked for exemptions on tariffs, but suggested that the Trump administration would not extend further carve outs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Dems' response to ISIS leader arrest 'disturbing,’ says father of Marine killed at Abbey Gate
item.description: Father of fallen Marine Jared Schmitz, who was killed at Abbey Gate by an ISIS-K bomber, was "disturbed" when Democrats refused to applaud the arrest of the terrorist involved in the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge rejects NYC's request to order immediate return of $80 million from FEMA to shelter migrants
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday ruled that the Federal Emergency Management Agency doesn’t need to immediately return more than $80 million that it took away from New York City last month in a dispute over funding for sheltering migrants. Judge Jennifer H.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Scientists around the globe raise concerns as the US stops sharing air quality data from embassies
item.description: The U.S. government’s decision to stop sharing air quality data from embassies and consulates worldwide has worried some local scientists and experts who say the data was vital for people to be aware of air quality and monitor their government's progress in improving public health.
item.dc:creator: Taiwo Adebayo And Sibi Arasu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump issues 'last warning' to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza
item.description: President Donald Trump has issued what he calls a “last warning” to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DOJ charges Chinese hackers, government officials in campaign targeting US agencies
item.description: The U.S. government has charged 10 Chinese hackers-for-hire and two of the country's security officials in a scheme to steal data from American entities and dissidents of the Chinese government, the Department of Justice (DOJ) announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: UN food agency says it has 2 weeks of supplies in Gaza
item.description: The U.N. World Food Program says it only has enough food supplies in the Gaza Strip to keep public kitchens and bakeries open for less than two weeks, after Israel halted the entry of food, fuel, medicine and other supplies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pentagon denies reports Hegseth ordered halt in cyber operations against Russia
item.description: The Department of Defense is denying reports Secretary Pete Hegseth halted offensive cyber operations against Russia.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: US charges Chinese hackers, government officials in broad cybercrime campaign
item.description: Twelve Chinese nationals, including mercenary hackers, law enforcement officers and employees of a private hacking company, have been charged in connection with global cybercrime campaigns targeting dissidents, news organizations, U.S. agencies and universities.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Kremlin says a 2022 Ukrainian decree bans Zelenskyy from talks with Putin
item.description: Russia on Wednesday asked how Ukraine could attend potential talks on ending their three-year war when a Ukrainian decree from 2022 rules out negotiations with President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trudeau not willing to lift Canada's retaliatory tariffs if Trump leaves some tariffs on Canada
item.description: A senior Canadian government official says Prime Minister Justin Trudeau is not willing to lift Canada’s retaliatory tariffs if Trump leaves any tariffs on Canada, The official confirmed the stance on condition of anonymity as they were not authorized to speak publicly on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: EU leaders to hold emergency Ukraine talks, seeking to adapt to new security demands without the US
item.description: European Union leaders plan to hold emergency talks to agree ways to quickly increase their military budgets after the Trump administration signaled Europe must take care of its own security and also suspended assistance to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook And Raf Casert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump pleads with people of Greenland to join the US
item.description: (The Hill) - President Trump on Tuesday said he’d welcome the people of Greenland to join the U.S. as he stressed that “I think we’re going to get” the self-governed Arctic territory within Denmark. "I also have a message tonight for the incredible people of Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Kabul airport bombing suspect arrives in US
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel confirmed that the suspect accused of orchestrating the 2021 Kabul airport bombing arrived in the U.S. on Tuesday to face charges for developing a plot that killed 13 American service members and at least 170 Afghan civilians.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: China will increase its defense budget 7.2% this year
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Wednesday it will increase its defense budget 7.2% this year, as it continues its campaign to build a larger, more modern military to assert its territorial claims and challenge the U.S. defense lead in Asia.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's plan to rebuild Gaza as an alternative to Trump's proposal
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders on Tuesday endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain, in a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to depopulate the territory and redevelop it as a beach destination.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump touts $5 million ‘gold card’ as new path to citizenship
item.description: President Donald Trump touts a $5million "gold card" as a "more sophisticated" green card to open up the path to citizenship that will attract the wealthiest students and entrepreneurs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope stable with no new respiratory crises but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Residents flee border area as Afghan and Pakistani forces clash over crossing closure
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Residents fled a border area between Afghanistan and Pakistan after forces from both sides clashed into the early hours of Tuesday, a Pakistani official said.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pressured by Trump’s Gaza plan, Arab countries meet in Egypt to discuss alternative proposal
item.description: Arab nations met for a summit in Cairo, Egypt, on Tuesday with the goal of developing a counterproposal to Trump's Gaza plan, aiming to rebuild without displacing residents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Department of Education clarifies school DEI guidelines, saying Black History Month doesn't violate order
item.description: The Department of Education clarified that celebrating Black History Month is not in violation of an order warning states to remove DEI policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With the Gaza ceasefire in limbo, Israel tries to impose an alternative plan on Hamas
item.description: Israel this week introduced what it said was a new U.S. ceasefire plan — different from the one it agreed to in January — and is trying to force Hamas to accept it by imposing a siege on the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis suffers 2 acute respiratory crises, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two episodes of acute respiratory distress Monday as his prognosis remains "guarded" while at a hospital in Rome, the Vatican announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis stable a day after respiratory crisis, spent 'long periods' off noninvasive ventilator
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition Saturday a day after a respiratory crisis during his battle with double pneumonia, the Vatican said. He spent long periods of the day off the noninvasive mechanical ventilation he initially needed in a sign that his lung function was improving.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9
